other_observers: none.; Seen by many others during and prior to the Pueblo Reservoir CBC.
description: Large, black-backed gull, mirrors on P9,P10, with the P10 mirror merged with the white tip to the primary. Eyes appearing pale brownish, heavy bill with prominent red spot on the gonys with some distal black smudging in the spot. Faint streaking around the eyes and back towards the nape. Pink legs, etc. See included photos.Behaviors: Seen in flight, then circling the tires at the south shore marina at Pueblo Reservoir. Twice (out of 4+ attempts) it went after Ring-billed gulls, tackling them into the water and biting at their bodies and shoulders for a few seconds until giving up and letting them free. This seemed to me to look more like predatory behavior than kleptoparasitism.Call: No vocalizations madePlumage: Adult basic
